question_text: A processor has one general purpose register, the Accumulator (ACC), and several special purpose registers. (a) Complete the following description of the role of the registers in the fetch-execute cycle by writing the missing registers. The holds the address of the next instruction to be loaded. [5] 7 9618/11/M/J/21 [Turn over (b) The following table shows part of the instruction set for a processor. The processor has one general purpose register, the Accumulator (ACC), and an Index Register (IX). Instruction Explanation Opcode Operand LDM #n Immediate addressing. Load the number n to ACC LDD <address> Direct addressing. Load the contents of the location at the given address to ACC LDI <address> Indirect addressing: The address to be used is at the given address. Load the contents of this second address to ACC LDX <address> Indexed addressing. Form the address from <address> + the contents of the Index Register. Copy the contents of this calculated address to ACC LDR #n Immediate addressing. Load the number n to IX MOV <register> Move the contents of the accumulator to the given register (IX) STO <address> Store contents of ACC at the given address ADD <address> Add the contents of the given address to the ACC INC <register> Add 1 to the contents of the register (ACC or IX) CMP <address> Compare the contents of ACC with the contents of <address> JPE <address> Following a compare instruction, jump to <address> if the compare was True JPN <address> Following a compare instruction, jump to <address> if the compare was False JMP <address> Jump to the given address OUT Output to the screen the character whose ASCII value is stored in ACC END Return control to the operating system LSL #n Bits in ACC are shifted logically n places to the left. subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: Melinda and her friends set up a peer-to-peer network between their computers to share data. (a) Describe the key features of a peer-to-peer network. [2] (b) Describe two drawbacks to Melinda and her friends of using a peer-to-peer network. 1 2 [4] (c) Melinda connects her laptop to the internet through her router. (i) Tick ( 3 ) one box in each row to identify whether the task is performed by the router or not. Task Performed by router Not performed by router Receives packets from devices Finds the IP address of a Uniform Resource Locator (URL) Directs each packet to all devices attached to it Stores the IP and/or MAC address of all devices attached to it [2] 12 9618/11/M/J/21 (ii) Melinda mainly uses the internet to watch films and play computer games. Tick ( 3 ) one box to identify whether Melinda should connect to the router using a wired or wireless network and justify your choice. Wired Wireless Justification [3] (d) Melinda sends emails from her webmail account (email account accessed through a website). Explain whether Melinda is using the internet, or the World Wide Web (WWW), or both. [3] 13 9618/11/M/J/21 [Turn over
mark_scheme_text:
4(a) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 ГўвӮ¬Вў All computers are of equal status ГўвӮ¬Вў Each computer provides access to resources and data // data is distributed ГўвӮ¬Вў Computers can communicate and share resources ГўвӮ¬Вў Each computer is responsible for its own security 2

4(b) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 per drawback ГўвӮ¬Вў Reduced security // no central management of security ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ only as secure as the weakest computer on the network ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ each computer is at risk from viruses from other computers ГўвӮ¬Вў No central management of backup ГўвӮ¬Вў ... if the data from one computer is not backed up it is lost to all of them ГўвӮ¬Вў No central management of files/software ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ consistency may be difficult to maintain ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ each computer may have different software from the others ГўвӮ¬Вў Individual computers may respond slower ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ because they are being accessed by other computers ГўвӮ¬Вў In order to share files etc. all the computers involved need to be switched on ГўвӮ¬Вў ... so the files etc. may not be always available 4 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 7 of 10 Question Answer Marks

4(c)(i) 1 mark for first 2 ticks, 1 mark for last 2 (shaded) Task Performed by router Not performed by router Receives packets from devices ГҜЖ’Вј Finds the IP address of a Uniform Resource Locator (URL) ГҜЖ’Вј Directs each packet to all devices attached to it ГҜЖ’Вј Stores the IP and/or MAC address of all devices attached to it ГҜЖ’Вј 2

4(c)(ii) 1 mark per bullet point for justification up to max 3 No mark for identification of wired/wireless Wired ГўвӮ¬Вў Faster connection // higher bandwidth ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ. needed as she is downloading/streaming large files ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ less time waiting / less latency / fewer delays ГўвӮ¬Вў More reliable / stable connection ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ is less susceptible to issues with distance/walls/interference ГўвӮ¬Вў More secure Wireless ГўвӮ¬Вў Freedom of movement ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ can move between different rooms with a mobile device and still receive/transmit data ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ no need of a physical connection ГўвӮ¬Вў Easily expanded if friends want to access the same network ГўвӮ¬Вў Less cabling / expertise is needed ГўвӮ¬Вў ... making the initial setup less expensive 3

4(d) 1 mark for identifying that she is using both. 1 mark per bullet point for justification ГўвӮ¬Вў using internet because sending data on the infrastructure ГўвӮ¬Вў using WWW because accessing a website (that is stored on a web server operated by the webmail) that is part of the WWW 3 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 8 of 10 Question Answer Marks

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: Kiara has a washing machine and a refrigerator. (a) She has an embedded system in her washing machine. Describe what is meant by an embedded system , using the washing machine as an example. [2] (b) The washing machineГўвӮ¬в„ўs embedded system makes use of both Random Access Memory (RAM) and Read Only Memory (ROM). State the purpose of RAM and ROM within the washing machineГўвӮ¬в„ўs embedded system. RAM ROM [2] (c) The temperature in her refrigerator must be kept between 4 and 6 degrees Celsius. The microprocessor in the refrigerator turns on the cooling if the temperature is too high, and turns off the cooling if the temperature is too low. Explain why the system in the refrigerator is a control and not a monitoring system. [2] 14 9618/11/M/J/21
mark_scheme_text:
5(a) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 ГўвӮ¬Вў Definition: Microprocessor/microcontroller within a larger system // microprocessor/microcontroller that performs one specific task ГўвӮ¬Вў Example: e.g. Embedded system in washing machine only controls the programs for the washing cycle // it is part of the washing machine but does not perform any other function within it 2

5(b) 1 mark for RAM, 1 mark for ROM RAM: ГўвӮ¬Вў Store the choices/wash program the user has entered // stores the data read from the sensors // stores the time left in the program // by example ROM: ГўвӮ¬Вў Store the start-up instructions (for the washing cycles) 2

5(c) 1 mark per bullet point ГўвӮ¬Вў The system uses feedback ГўвӮ¬Вў The system causes the temperature to change // produces an action 2 Question Answer Marks

7(a) 1 mark per bullet point to max 3 ГўвӮ¬Вў Flat-file has more data redundancy ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ because the same data is stored many times // data is stored in different tables which are linked ГўвӮ¬Вў There is program-data dependence with flat-files ГўвӮ¬Вў ... because any changes to the structure of the data means the programs that access that data have to be re-written ГўвӮ¬Вў Flat-file has more data inconsistency // worse data integrity ГўвӮ¬Вў ... because duplicated data might be stored differently //ГўвӮ¬ВҰbecause when data is updated in one place, it is not updated everywhere ГўвӮ¬Вў It is not easy to perform complex searches /queries ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ because a new program has to be written each time ГўвӮ¬Вў Flat files could have a lack of privacy ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ as user views cannot easily be implemented 3

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: Seth uses a computer for work. (a) Complete the following descriptions of internal components of a computer by writing the missing terms. The transmits the signals to coordinate events based on the electronic pulses of the . The carries data to the components, while the carries the address where data needs to be written to or read from. The performs mathematical operations and logical comparisons. [5] (b) Describe the ways in which the following factors can affect the performance of his laptop computer. Number of cores Clock speed [4] 10 9618/12/M/J/21 (c) Seth accesses both software and data using cloud computing. (i) Give two benefits of storing data using cloud computing. 1 2 [2] (ii) Give two drawbacks of Seth using cloud computing. 1 2 [2] 11 9618/12/M/J/21 [Turn over (d) Draw one line from each term to its most appropriate description. Term Description Public IP address Private IP address Dynamic IP address Static IP address It is only visible to devices within the Local Area Network (LAN) It increments by 1 each time the device connects to the internet A new one is reallocated each time a device connects to the internet It can only be allocated to a router It is visible to any device on the internet It does not change each time a device connects to the internet [4] 12 9618/12/M/J/21
mark_scheme_text:
5(a) 1 mark for each term correctly inserted The control unit/bus transmits the signals to coordinate events based on the pulses of the ( system) clock . The data bus carries data to components, while the address bus carries the address where data is being written to or read from. The arithmetic logic unit/ALU performs mathematical operations and logical comparisons. 5

5(b) 1 mark per bullet point to max 3 per factor. max 4 overall. Number of cores: ГўвӮ¬Вў Each core processes one instruction per clock pulse ГўвӮ¬Вў More/multiple cores mean that sequences of instructions can be split between them ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ and so more than one instruction is executed per clock pulse // more sequences of instructions can be run at the same time ГўвӮ¬Вў More cores decreases the time taken to complete task Clock speed: ГўвӮ¬Вў Each instruction is executed on a clock pulse // one F-E cycle is run on each clock pulse ГўвӮ¬Вў ... so the clock speed dictates the number of instructions that can be run per second ГўвӮ¬Вў The faster the clock speed the more instructions can be run per second 4

5(c)(i) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 ГўвӮ¬Вў Cloud storage can be free (for small quantities ) ГўвӮ¬Вў No need for separate (high capacity) storage devices // saves storage on existing devices ГўвӮ¬Вў Can access data from any computer with internet access ГўвӮ¬Вў Most cloud data services will have in-built backup/disaster recovery ГўвӮ¬Вў Security could be better ГўвӮ¬Вў Can easily increase capacity ГўвӮ¬Вў Data can be easily shared 2 9618/12 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 8 of 10 Question Answer Marks

5(c)(ii) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 : ГўвӮ¬Вў Can only access (the cloud) with internet access ГўвӮ¬Вў Security may not be strong // no control over security ГўвӮ¬Вў There may not be any backups // no control over backups ГўвӮ¬Вў It can take a long time to upload/download the data ГўвӮ¬Вў It can be more expensive in the long term ГўвӮ¬Вў There could be a limit to the amount of storage unless paid for ГўвӮ¬Вў There could be compatibility/access issues ГўвӮ¬Вў There could be issues with the company offering cloud services 2

5(d) 1 mark for each correct line 4 Question Answer Marks

4(b) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 per drawback ГўвӮ¬Вў Reduced security // no central management of security ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ only as secure as the weakest computer on the network ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ each computer is at risk from viruses from other computers ГўвӮ¬Вў No central management of backup ГўвӮ¬Вў ... if the data from one computer is not backed up it is lost to all of them ГўвӮ¬Вў No central management of files/software ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ consistency may be difficult to maintain ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ each computer may have different software from the others ГўвӮ¬Вў Individual computers may respond slower ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ because they are being accessed by other computers ГўвӮ¬Вў In order to share files etc. all the computers involved need to be switched on ГўвӮ¬Вў ... so the files etc. may not be always available 4 9618/13 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 7 of 10 Question Answer Marks

7(a) 1 mark per bullet point to max 3 ГўвӮ¬Вў Flat-file has more data redundancy ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ because the same data is stored many times // data is stored in different tables which are linked ГўвӮ¬Вў There is program-data dependence with flat-files ГўвӮ¬Вў ... because any changes to the structure of the data means the programs that access that data have to be re-written ГўвӮ¬Вў Flat-file has more data inconsistency // worse data integrity ГўвӮ¬Вў ... because duplicated data might be stored differently //ГўвӮ¬ВҰbecause when data is updated in one place, it is not updated everywhere ГўвӮ¬Вў It is not easy to perform complex searches /queries ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ because a new program has to be written each time ГўвӮ¬Вў Flat files could have a lack of privacy ГўвӮ¬Вў ГўвӮ¬ВҰ as user views cannot easily be implemented 3

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A concert venue uses a program to calculate admission prices and store information about ticket sales. A number of arrays are used to store data. The computer is switched off overnight and data has to be input again at the start of each day before any tickets can be sold. This process is very time consuming. (i) Explain how the program could use text files to speed up the process. [2] (ii) State the characteristic of text files that allow them to be used as explained in part (a)(i) . [1] (iii) Information about ticket sales will be stored as a booking. The booking requires the following data: ГўвӮ¬Вў name of person booking ГўвӮ¬Вў number of people in the group (for example a family ticket or a school party) ГўвӮ¬Вў event type. Suggest how data relating to each booking may be stored in a text file. [2] 9 9618/21/M/J/21 [Turn over (b) A procedure Preview() will: ГўвӮ¬Вў take the name of a text file as a parameter ГўвӮ¬Вў output a warning message if the file is empty ГўвӮ¬Вў otherwise output the first five lines from the file (or as many lines as there are in the file if this number is less than five). Write pseudocode for the procedure Preview() . [5] 10 9618/21/M/J/21
mark_scheme_text:
3(a)(i) One mark per bullet point: ГўвӮ¬Вў Data from the arrays is written to the files at the end of the day / before the program is terminated / computer is switched off ГўвӮ¬Вў Data can then be read from the files at the start of the next day and written to / stored in the arrays ГўвӮ¬Вў No need to (re-)enter the data manually // only need to enter data once Note: Max 2 marks 2

3(a)(ii) ГўвӮ¬Вў The data is retained when the program is terminated / after the computer is switched off // data is stored permanently // non-volatile storage 1

3(a)(iii) One mark per bullet point: ГўвӮ¬Вў Data items are combined to form a single string / saved as a single line in the file ГўвӮ¬Вў Data items are separated by a special character // make each data item a fixed length ALTERNATIVE: ГўвӮ¬Вў Convert all data items / 'number of people' to strings ГўвӮ¬Вў Consecutive / each line stores a separate data item 2 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 8 of 16 Question Answer Marks

3(b) PROCEDURE Preview (ThisFile : STRING) DECLARE LineNum : INTEGER DECLARE ThisLine : STRING OPENFILE ThisFile FOR READ IF EOF(ThisFile) THEN OUTPUT ГўвӮ¬Е“Warning MessageГўвӮ¬Вқ ELSE LineNum ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 WHILE LineNum < 6 AND NOT EOF(ThisFile) READFILE Thisfile, ThisLine OUTPUT ThisLine LineNum ГўвҖ Вҗ LineNum + 1 ENDWHILE ENDIF CLOSEFILE ThisFile ENDPROCEDURE Marks as follows: 1 Procedure heading (including parameter) and ending 2 File OPEN and subsequently CLOSE 3 Check if file is empty and output a warning message if it is 4 Conditional Loop 5 Output line (including blank lines) and read next line in a loop 5 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 9 of 16 Question Answer Marks

4(c)(ii) One mark for each: Line number: 26 New position: Move to after line 27 / line 28 2 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 11 of 16 Question Answer Marks 5 PROCEDURE Sort() DECLARE Temp : INTEGER DECLARE NoSwaps : BOOLEAN DECLARE Boundary, Row, Col : INTEGER Boundary ГўвҖ Вҗ 999 REPEAT NoSwaps ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE FOR Row ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 TO Boundary IF Result[Row, 2] > Result[Row + 1, 2] THEN FOR Col ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 TO 2 Temp ГўвҖ Вҗ Result [Row, Col] Result [Row, Col] ГўвҖ Вҗ Result [Row + 1, Col] Result [Row + 1, Col] ГўвҖ Вҗ Temp NEXT Col NoSwaps ГўвҖ Вҗ FALSE ENDIF NEXT J Boundary ГўвҖ Вҗ Boundary - 1 UNTIL NoSwaps = TRUE ENDPROCEDURE Mark as follows: 1 Outer loop 2 Inner loop 3 Correct comparison in a loop 4 Correct swap of col1 array elements in a loop 5 Correct swap of col2 array elements in a loop (via loop or separate statements) 6 'NoSwap' mechanism: Conditional outer loop including flag reset 7 'NoSwap' mechanism: Set flag in inner loop to indicate swap 8 Reducing Boundary in the outer loop 8 Question Answer Marks

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A program is needed to take a string containing a full name and produce a new string of initials. Some words in the full name will be ignored. For example, "the", "and", "of", "for" and "to" may all be ignored. Each letter of the abbreviated string must be upper case. For example: Full name Initials Integrated Development Environment IDE The American Standard Code for Information Interchange ASCII The programmer has decided to use a global variable FNString of type STRING to store the full name. It is assumed that: ГўвӮ¬Вў words in the full name string are separated by a single space character ГўвӮ¬Вў space characters will not occur at the beginning or the end of the full name string ГўвӮ¬Вў the full name string contains at least one word. The programmer has started to define program modules as follows: Module Description GetStart() ГўвӮ¬Вў Called with an INTEGER as a parameter, representing the number of a word in FNString . ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the character start position of that word in FNString or returns ГўвӮ¬вҖң1 if that word does not exist ГўвӮ¬Вў For example: if FNString contains the string "hot and cold" , GetStart(3) returns
mark_scheme_text:
7(a) FUNCTION GetStart (WordNum : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE Index, ThisPos, NumFound : INTEGER DECLARE ThisChar : Char CONSTANT SPACECHAR = ' ' Index ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 Numfound ГўвҖ Вҗ 0 ThisPos ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 IF WordNum = 1 THEN // if looking for word 1... Index ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 // Word 1 always starts at index // position 1 ELSE // Otherwise start counting spaces... WHILE ThisPos <= LENGTH(FNString) AND Index = -1 ThisChar ГўвҖ Вҗ MID(FNString, ThisPos, 1) IF ThisChar = SPACECHAR THEN NumFound ГўвҖ Вҗ NumFound + 1 IF NumFound = WordNum - 1 THEN Index ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisPos + 1 // the start of the // required word ENDIF ENDIF ThisPos ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisPos + 1 ENDWHILE ENDIF RETURN Index ENDFUNCTION 1 mark for each of the following: 1 Function heading, including return type and function end 2 Loop counting spaces until word found or end of FNString 3 extract a character from FNString in a loop 4 compare with SPACECHAR and increment count if equal in a loop 5 compare count with WordNum - 1 (depending on initialisation value) in a loop 6 if equal then set flag or Index to ThisPos + 1 in a loop 7 Return Index (correctly in all cases / following a reasonable attempt) 8 Works for special case when looking for word 1 Note: Max 7 marks 7 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 14 of 16 Question Answer Marks

7(b) Marks awarded for any reference to each of the following steps of the algorithm: 1 Mention of variable for use as array index 2 Use of a loop (to check through the array) 3 If word is the same as the current array element then return FALSE / set flag 4 If word not already in array, loop to find unused element (second loop) 5 Store word in unused element and return TRUE , otherwise return FALSE VARIATION: 1 Mention of variable for use as array index 2 Use of a loop (to check through the array) 3 Save index of (first) unused element found 4 If word is the same as the current array element then return FALSE / set flag 5 If word not already in array and unused element available, store word in unused element and return TRUE otherwise return FALSE Note: Max 4 marks 4 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 15 of 16 Question Answer Marks

7(c) FUNCTION GetWord (Index : INTEGER) RETURNS STRING DECLARE NextWord : STRING DECLARE Done : BOOLEAN DECLARE ThisChar : CHAR DECLARE Index : INTEGER CONSTANT SPACECHAR = ' ' NextWord ГўвҖ Вҗ "" Done ГўвҖ Вҗ FALSE REPEAT ThisChar ГўвҖ Вҗ MID(FNString, Index, 1) IF ThisChar <> SPACECHAR THEN NextWord ГўвҖ Вҗ NextWord & ThisChar // build up NextWord ENDIF IF ThisChar = SPACECHAR OR Index = LENGTH(FNString) THEN Done ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE ENDIF Index ГўвҖ Вҗ Index + 1 UNTIL Done = TRUE RETURN NextWord ENDFUNCTION 1 mark for each of the following: 1 Conditional loop 2 Extract char from FNString and compare with SPACECHAR in a loop 3 Concatenate with NextWord if not SPACECHAR in a loop 4 Exit loop when SPACECHAR encountered or when end of FNString reached 5 Return NextWord (after reasonable attempt at forming, and must have been initialised)

question_text: A program is needed to take a string containing a full name and to produce a new string of initials. Some words in the full name will be ignored. For example, ГўвӮ¬Е“theГўвӮ¬Вқ, ГўвӮ¬Е“andГўвӮ¬Вқ, ГўвӮ¬Е“ofГўвӮ¬Вқ, ГўвӮ¬Е“forГўвӮ¬Вқ and ГўвӮ¬Е“toГўвӮ¬Вқ may all be ignored. Each letter of the new string must be upper case. For example: Full name Initials Integrated Development Environment IDE The American Standard Code for Information Interchange ASCII The programmer has decided to use the following global variables: ГўвӮ¬Вў a ten element 1D array IgnoreList of type STRING to store the ignored words ГўвӮ¬Вў a string FNString to store the full name string. Assume that: ГўвӮ¬Вў each alphabetic character in the full name string may be either upper or lower case ГўвӮ¬Вў the full name string contains at least one word. The programmer has started to define program modules as follows: Module Description GetStart() ГўвӮ¬Вў Called with an INTEGER as its parameter, representing the number of a word in FNString ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the character start position of that word in FNString or returns -1 if that word does not exist ГўвӮ¬Вў For example: GetStart(3) applied to "hot and cold" returns
mark_scheme_text:
8(a) FUNCTION IgnoreWord (ThisWord : STRING) RETURNS BOOLEAN DECLARE Found : BOOLEAN DECLARE Index : INTEGER Found ГўвҖ Вҗ False Index ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 ThisWord ГўвҖ Вҗ TO_LOWER(ThisWord) REPEAT IF TO_LOWER(IgnoreList[Index]) = ThisWord THEN Found ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE ENDIF Index ГўвҖ Вҗ Index + 1 UNTIL Found = TRUE OR Index > 10 RETURN Found ENDFUNCTION 1 mark for each of the following: 1 Loop through array elements 2 Convert both strings to same case 3 Compare array element with parameter in a loop 4 Set a flag (or similar) if match found (after reasonable attempt at MP3) in a loop 5 Return TRUE or FALSE in all cases Note: Max 4 if function declaration incorrect 5 9618/22 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 10 of 10 Question Answer Marks

8(b) Procedure GetInitials() DECLARE NewString, NextWord : STRING DECLARE ThisWordNum, Index : INTEGER ThisWordNum ГўвҖ Вҗ 0 NewString ГўвҖ Вҗ "" REPEAT ThisWordNum ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisWordNum + 1 Index ГўвҖ Вҗ GetStart(ThisWordNum) IF Index <> -1 THEN //if there is ThisWordNum NextWord ГўвҖ Вҗ GetWord(Index) IF IgnoreWord(NextWord) = FALSE THEN NewString ГўвҖ Вҗ NewString & UCASE(LEFT(NextWord, 1)) ENDIF ENDIF UNTIL Index = -1 OUTPUT NewString ENDPROCEDURE 1 mark for each of the following: 1 Declare NewString and initialise to empty string 2 Conditional loop to pick out all words from FNString 3 Evaluate result of GetStart() in a loop 4 Test result <> -1 and if not: 5 Assign result of GetWord() to a variable in a loop 6 Test result of IgnoreWord() in a loop 7 If not ignored, add the next initial letter to NewString in a loop 8 Increment ThisWordNum (must have been initialised) in a loop 9 Output NewString (must be all upper case) outside loop Note: Max 8 marks 8

3(b) PROCEDURE Preview (ThisFile : STRING) DECLARE LineNum : INTEGER DECLARE ThisLine : STRING OPENFILE ThisFile FOR READ IF EOF(ThisFile) THEN OUTPUT ГўвӮ¬Е“Warning MessageГўвӮ¬Вқ ELSE LineNum ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 WHILE LineNum < 6 AND NOT EOF(ThisFile) READFILE Thisfile, ThisLine OUTPUT ThisLine LineNum ГўвҖ Вҗ LineNum + 1 ENDWHILE ENDIF CLOSEFILE ThisFile ENDPROCEDURE Marks as follows: 1 Procedure heading (including parameter) and ending 2 File OPEN and subsequently CLOSE 3 Check if file is empty and output a warning message if it is 4 Conditional Loop 5 Output line (including blank lines) and read next line in a loop 5 9618/23 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 9 of 16 Question Answer Marks

7(a) FUNCTION GetStart (WordNum : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE Index, ThisPos, NumFound : INTEGER DECLARE ThisChar : Char CONSTANT SPACECHAR = ' ' Index ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 Numfound ГўвҖ Вҗ 0 ThisPos ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 IF WordNum = 1 THEN // if looking for word 1... Index ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 // Word 1 always starts at index // position 1 ELSE // Otherwise start counting spaces... WHILE ThisPos <= LENGTH(FNString) AND Index = -1 ThisChar ГўвҖ Вҗ MID(FNString, ThisPos, 1) IF ThisChar = SPACECHAR THEN NumFound ГўвҖ Вҗ NumFound + 1 IF NumFound = WordNum - 1 THEN Index ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisPos + 1 // the start of the // required word ENDIF ENDIF ThisPos ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisPos + 1 ENDWHILE ENDIF RETURN Index ENDFUNCTION 1 mark for each of the following: 1 Function heading, including return type and function end 2 Loop counting spaces until word found or end of FNString 3 extract a character from FNString in a loop 4 compare with SPACECHAR and increment count if equal in a loop 5 compare count with WordNum - 1 (depending on initialisation value) in a loop 6 if equal then set flag or Index to ThisPos + 1 in a loop 7 Return Index (correctly in all cases / following a reasonable attempt) 8 Works for special case when looking for word 1 Note: Max 7 marks 7 9618/23 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 14 of 16 Question Answer Marks

7(b) Marks awarded for any reference to each of the following steps of the algorithm: 1 Mention of variable for use as array index 2 Use of a loop (to check through the array) 3 If word is the same as the current array element then return FALSE / set flag 4 If word not already in array, loop to find unused element (second loop) 5 Store word in unused element and return TRUE , otherwise return FALSE VARIATION: 1 Mention of variable for use as array index 2 Use of a loop (to check through the array) 3 Save index of (first) unused element found 4 If word is the same as the current array element then return FALSE / set flag 5 If word not already in array and unused element available, store word in unused element and return TRUE otherwise return FALSE Note: Max 4 marks 4 9618/23 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 15 of 16 Question Answer Marks

7(c) FUNCTION GetWord (Index : INTEGER) RETURNS STRING DECLARE NextWord : STRING DECLARE Done : BOOLEAN DECLARE ThisChar : CHAR DECLARE Index : INTEGER CONSTANT SPACECHAR = ' ' NextWord ГўвҖ Вҗ "" Done ГўвҖ Вҗ FALSE REPEAT ThisChar ГўвҖ Вҗ MID(FNString, Index, 1) IF ThisChar <> SPACECHAR THEN NextWord ГўвҖ Вҗ NextWord & ThisChar // build up NextWord ENDIF IF ThisChar = SPACECHAR OR Index = LENGTH(FNString) THEN Done ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE ENDIF Index ГўвҖ Вҗ Index + 1 UNTIL Done = TRUE RETURN NextWord ENDFUNCTION 1 mark for each of the following: 1 Conditional loop 2 Extract char from FNString and compare with SPACECHAR in a loop 3 Concatenate with NextWord if not SPACECHAR in a loop 4 Exit loop when SPACECHAR encountered or when end of FNString reached 5 Return NextWord (after reasonable attempt at forming, and must have been initialised)

They produce executable/.exe/object code files that can be run without the source code. Interpreters translate one line of a high-level language program at a time, and then run that line of code. question_text: The Von Neumann model for a computer system uses registers. (a) Describe the role of the following special purpose registers in the fetch-execute (F-E) cycle. The processor has one general purpose register, the Accumulator (ACC), and an Index Register (IX). Instruction Explanation Opcode Operand LDM #n Immediate addressing. Load the number n to ACC LDD <address> Direct addressing. Load the contents of the location at the given address to ACC STO <address> Store the contents of ACC at the given address INC <register> Add 1 to the contents of the register (ACC or IX) CMP <address> Compare the contents of ACC with the contents of <address> JPN <address> Following a compare instruction, jump to <address> if the compare was False JMP <address> Jump to the given address IN Key in a character and store its ASCII value in ACC OUT Output to the screen the character whose ASCII value is stored in ACC END Return control to the operating system XOR #n Bitwise XOR operation of the contents of ACC with the operand XOR <address> Bitwise XOR operation of the contents of ACC with the contents of <address> OR #n Bitwise OR operation of the contents of ACC with the operand OR <address> Bitwise OR operation of the contents of ACC with the contents of <address> AND #n Bitwise AND operation of the contents of ACC with the operand AND <address> Bitwise AND operation of the contents of ACC with the contents of <address> LSL #n Bits in ACC are shifted logically n places to the left. Zeros are introduced on the right hand end LSR #n Bits in ACC are shifted logically n places to the right. Zeros are introduced on the left hand end <address> can be an absolute or symbolic address # denotes a denary number, e.g. #123 The current contents of main memory are shown: Address Data 100 01010101 101 11110000 102 00001111 103 00000000 104 11111111 15 9618/12/O/N/21 [Turn over (i) In the following table, each row shows the current contents of the ACC in binary and the instruction that will be performed on those contents. Instruction Group Instruction Data movement LDM #

question_text: Xanthe wants to maintain the integrity and security of data stored on her computer. (a) Explain the difference between data security and data integrity. subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: Mouse End of queue pointer A mobile phone has a touchscreen. The screen is represented by a grid, divided into 800 rows and 1280 columns. The grid is represented by a 2D array Screen of type INTEGER . An array element will be set to 0 unless the user touches that part of the screen. Many array elements are set to 1 by a single touch of a finger or a stylus. The following diagram shows a simplified touchscreen. The dark line represents a touch to the screen. All grid elements that are wholly or partly inside the outline will be set to 1. These elements are shaded. The element shaded in black represents the centre point. 11 A program is needed to find the coordinates (the row and column) of the centre point. The centre point on the diagram above is row 6, column 11. Assume: ГўвӮ¬Вў the user may only touch one area at a time ГўвӮ¬Вў screen rotation does not affect the touchscreen. The programmer has started to define program modules as follows: Module Description SetRow() (generates test data) ГўвӮ¬Вў Called with three parameters of type INTEGER : ГўвҖ”ВҰ a row number ГўвҖ”ВҰ the number of pixels to be skipped starting from column 1 ГўвҖ”ВҰ the number of pixels that should be set to 1 ГўвӮ¬Вў Sets the required number of pixels to
mark_scheme_text:
6(a) PROCEDURE SetRow(Row, SkipNum, SetNum : INTEGER) DECLARE Col : INTEGER // array is 1280 x 800 FOR Col ГўвҖ Вҗ SkipNum + 1 TO SkipNum + SetNum Screen[Row, Col] ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 NEXT Index ENDPROCEDURE ALTERNATIVE 1: FOR Col ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 TO SetNum Screen[Row, SkipNum + Col] ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 NEXT Col ALTERNATIVE 2: WHILE SetNum > 0 Screen[Row, SkipNum + SetNum] ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 SetNum ГўвҖ Вҗ SetNum - 1 ENDWHILE Mark as follows: 1 Procedure heading and ending including parameters 2 Declaration of local Integer for Col 3 Count-controlled loop with meaningful start number 4 correct stop number 5 Reference Screen Array element and set to 1 in a loop 5 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2021 Page 10 of 11 Question Answer Marks

6(b) FUNCTION SearchInRow(ThisRow, StartCol : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE ThisCol, Step : INTEGER DECLARE Found: BOOLEAN // array is 1280 x 800 Found ГўвҖ Вҗ FALSE ThisCol ГўвҖ Вҗ StartCol // first decide which way to search IF StartCol = 1 THEN Step ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ 1281 ELSE Step ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ 0 ENDIF WHILE ThisCol <> EndCol AND Found = FALSE IF Screen[ThisRow, ThisCol] <> 1 THEN ThisCol ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisCol + Step ELSE Found ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE ENDIF ENDWHILE IF Found = FALSE THEN ThisCol ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 ENDIF RETURN ThisCol ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Interpreting StartCol parameter to determine direction of search 2 An attempt at searching both up and down 3 Conditional Loop / Count-controlled loop with use of ThisCol index 4 Using correct values for StartCol , EndCol and Step 5 Reference a Screen element and compare with 1 in a loop 6 If equal save column or immediately Return column in a loop 7 Return column number or ГўЛҶвҖҷ 1 Loop(s) terminate when element with value = 1 found Max 7 marks if function heading, including return type, and ending is incorrect or incomplete 8 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2021 Page 11 of 11 Question Answer Marks

6(c) FUNCTION GetCentreCol(ThisRow : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE StartCol, EndCol, CentreCol : INTEGER StartCol ГўвҖ Вҗ SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1) IF StartCol = -1 THEN CentreCol ГўвҖ Вҗ StartCol ELSE EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1280) CentreCol ГўвҖ Вҗ INT((StartCol + EndCol)/2) ENDIF RETURN CentreCol ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Declaration of local INTEGER for return value 2 Use SearchInRow() with correct parameters and check for -1 3 Use SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1) and SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1280) 4 Calculate centre column 5 Use of INT() function // use of DIV 6 Return ГўвӮ¬вҖң1 or centre value Max 5 marks if function heading, including return type, and ending is incorrect or incomplete 6

6(a) FUNCTION FirstRowSet() RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE Row, Col : INTEGER DECLARE Found : BOOLEAN // array is 1280 ГғвҖ” 800 Row ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 Found ГўвҖ Вҗ FALSE WHILE Row <= 800 AND Found = FALSE // top to bottom Col ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 WHILE Col <= 1280 AND Found = FALSE // left to right IF Screen[Row,Col] = 1 THEN Found ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE // end function as soon as first // found ENDIF Col ГўвҖ Вҗ Col + 1 ENDWHILE Row ГўвҖ Вҗ Row + 1 ENDWHILE IF Found = FALSE THEN // nothing found Row ГўвҖ Вҗ 0 ENDIF RETURN Row - 1 ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Function heading and ending and return type 2 (Conditional) outer loop 1 to 800 (row) 3 (Conditional) inner loop 1 to 1280 // 1280 to 1 (column) 4 Reference Screen element and test for = 1 // <> 0 5 and if true save row number and exit loops 6 Increment index variables in both inner and outer loop 7 Return Row number or ГўЛҶвҖҷ 1, following a reasonable attempt 7

6(b) One mark for: ГўвӮ¬Вў (A flag is used to) exit the loops // iteration is terminated ГўвӮ¬Вў as soon as a Screen element with value 1 is found 2 9618/22 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2021 Page 10 of 10 Question Answer Marks

6(c)(ii) One mark for advantage and one for disadvantage: Advantage: (max 1) ГўвӮ¬Вў Only have to change one module if specification changes ГўвӮ¬Вў Less repetitive code / fewer lines of code ГўвӮ¬Вў Aids re-usability Disadvantage: (max 1) ГўвӮ¬Вў Single module more complex / more error prone / more difficult to debug ... ГўвӮ¬Вў Single module cannot be split among programmers / teams Max 2 2

6(d) PROCEDURE GetCentre () DECLARE StartRow, EndRow, StartCol, EndCol : INTEGER StartRow ГўвҖ Вҗ FirstRowSet() IF StartRow = -1 THEN CentreRow ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 // no 'touch' detected ELSE EndRow ГўвҖ Вҗ LastRowSet() StartCol ГўвҖ Вҗ FirstColSet() EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ LastColSet() CentreRow ГўвҖ Вҗ INT((StartRow + EndRow)/2) CentreCol ГўвҖ Вҗ INT((StartCol + EndCol)/2) ENDIF ENDPROCEDURE Mark as follows: 1 Call <any Set function> and check for -1 // check for no element set 2 ...and if so set CentreRow to ГўвӮ¬вҖң1 3 Call all 4 Set functions to get 'extremity' values 4 Calculate centre row and centre column 5 Use of INT() function or DIV operator on values from MP4 6 Assign calculated values to CentreRow and CentreCol Note: Max 5 if procedure heading and ending missing or incorrect (ignore array if passed as a parameter) or any local variables are undefined or of incorrect type 6

6(a) PROCEDURE SetRow(Row, SkipNum, SetNum : INTEGER) DECLARE Col : INTEGER // array is 1280 x 800 FOR Col ГўвҖ Вҗ SkipNum + 1 TO SkipNum + SetNum Screen[Row, Col] ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 NEXT Index ENDPROCEDURE ALTERNATIVE 1: FOR Col ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 TO SetNum Screen[Row, SkipNum + Col] ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 NEXT Col ALTERNATIVE 2: WHILE SetNum > 0 Screen[Row, SkipNum + SetNum] ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 SetNum ГўвҖ Вҗ SetNum - 1 ENDWHILE Mark as follows: 1 Procedure heading and ending including parameters 2 Declaration of local Integer for Col 3 Count-controlled loop with meaningful start number 4 correct stop number 5 Reference Screen Array element and set to 1 in a loop 5 9618/23 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2021 Page 10 of 11 Question Answer Marks

6(b) FUNCTION SearchInRow(ThisRow, StartCol : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE ThisCol, Step : INTEGER DECLARE Found: BOOLEAN // array is 1280 x 800 Found ГўвҖ Вҗ FALSE ThisCol ГўвҖ Вҗ StartCol // first decide which way to search IF StartCol = 1 THEN Step ГўвҖ Вҗ 1 EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ 1281 ELSE Step ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ 0 ENDIF WHILE ThisCol <> EndCol AND Found = FALSE IF Screen[ThisRow, ThisCol] <> 1 THEN ThisCol ГўвҖ Вҗ ThisCol + Step ELSE Found ГўвҖ Вҗ TRUE ENDIF ENDWHILE IF Found = FALSE THEN ThisCol ГўвҖ Вҗ -1 ENDIF RETURN ThisCol ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Interpreting StartCol parameter to determine direction of search 2 An attempt at searching both up and down 3 Conditional Loop / Count-controlled loop with use of ThisCol index 4 Using correct values for StartCol , EndCol and Step 5 Reference a Screen element and compare with 1 in a loop 6 If equal save column or immediately Return column in a loop 7 Return column number or ГўЛҶвҖҷ 1 Loop(s) terminate when element with value = 1 found Max 7 marks if function heading, including return type, and ending is incorrect or incomplete 8 9618/23 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2021 Page 11 of 11 Question Answer Marks

6(c) FUNCTION GetCentreCol(ThisRow : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER DECLARE StartCol, EndCol, CentreCol : INTEGER StartCol ГўвҖ Вҗ SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1) IF StartCol = -1 THEN CentreCol ГўвҖ Вҗ StartCol ELSE EndCol ГўвҖ Вҗ SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1280) CentreCol ГўвҖ Вҗ INT((StartCol + EndCol)/2) ENDIF RETURN CentreCol ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Declaration of local INTEGER for return value 2 Use SearchInRow() with correct parameters and check for -1 3 Use SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1) and SearchInRow(ThisRow, 1280) 4 Calculate centre column 5 Use of INT() function // use of DIV 6 Return ГўвӮ¬вҖң1 or centre value Max 5 marks if function heading, including return type, and ending is incorrect or incomplete 6

question_text: Study the following pseudocode for a recursive function. FUNCTION Unknown(BYVAL X, BYVAL Y : INTEGER) RETURNS INTEGER IF X < Y THEN OUTPUT X + Y RETURN (Unknown(X + 1, Y) * 2) ELSE IF X = Y THEN RETURN ELSE OUTPUT X + Y RETURN (Unknown(X - 1, Y) DIV 2) ENDIF ENDIF ENDFUNCTION The operator DIV returns the integer value after division e.g. Each row in the table represents one node on the tree. The number -1 represents a null pointer. (a) The 2D array, ArrayNodes , is declared with space for 20 nodes. Each node has a left pointer, data and right pointer. The program also initialises the: ГўвӮ¬Вў RootPointer to - (null); this points to the first node in the binary tree ГўвӮ¬Вў FreeNode to 0 ; this points to the first empty node in the array. Write program code to declare ArrayNodes , RootPointer and FreeNode in the main program. If you are writing in Python programming language, include attribute declarations using comments. Save your program as question 3 . Copy and paste the program code into part 3(a) in the evidence document. [4] (b) The procedure AddNode() adds a new node to the array ArrayNodes . question_text: A program, written using object-oriented programming, stores pictures as objects. subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A computer has hardware and software. (a) The hardware includes different types of memory. (i) Complete the description of computer memory. Random Access Memory (RAM) and Read Only Memory (ROM) are both examples of memory. One item that is stored in RAM is . One item that is stored in ROM is . RAM can be either Static RAM (SRAM) or Dynamic RAM (DRAM). SRAM uses transistors arranged as . DRAM uses transistors and . [5] (ii) Explain the difference between Programmable ROM (PROM), Erasable Programmable ROM (EPROM) and Electrically Erasable Programmable ROM (EEPROM). [3] 4 9618/11/M/J/22 (b) A magnetic hard disk is used to store data on the computer. Describe the principal operations of a magnetic hard disk. [5] 5 9618/11/M/J/22 [Turn over (c) Computers consist of logic gates. (i) Complete the table by writing one set of values (input 1 and input 2) for each gate that will give the output 1. Gate Input Description Security measure [6] 7 9618/11/M/J/22 [Turn over
mark_scheme_text:
2(a)(i) 1 mark for each correct term. Random Access Memory (RAM) and Read Only Memory (ROM) are both examples of primary memory. One item that is stored in RAM is currently running software/data/part of OS . One item that is stored in ROM is the start-up/boot-up instructions/BIOS . RAM can be either Static RAM (SRAM) or Dynamic RAM (DRAM). SRAM uses transistors arranged as flip-flops/latches. DRAM uses transistors and capacitors . 5

2(a)(ii) 1 mark per bullet point to max 3 ГҜвҖҡВ· PROM can be set once, EPROM and EEPROM can be overwritten multiple times. ГҜвҖҡВ· EPROM needs to be removed from device EEPROM can be erased in situ. ГҜвҖҡВ· EPROM and can be erased using UV light, EEPROM can be erased using voltage // is flash storage . ГҜвҖҡВ· EPROM must be entirely erased before rewriting, EEPROM does not have to be entirely erased before rewriting. 3 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 4 of 8 Question Answer Marks

2(b) 1 mark per bullet point to max 5 ГҜвҖҡВ· The hard disk has (one or more) platter/plate/disk ГҜвҖҡВ· Each surface of the platter/disk is (ferrous oxide which is) capable of being magnetised ГҜвҖҡВ· The platters/disks are mounted on a (central) spindle ГҜвҖҡВ· The entire mechanism is contained inside a sealed (aluminium) box. ГҜвҖҡВ· The disks are rotated (at high-speed) ГҜвҖҡВ· (Each surface of the disk) has a read/write head mounted on an arm (positioned just above the surface) ГҜвҖҡВ· Electronic circuits control the movement of the arm (and hence the heads) ГҜвҖҡВ· The surface of the platter/disk is divided into concentric tracks / circles ГҜвҖҡВ· The surface of the platter/disk is divided into sectors ГҜвҖҡВ· One track in one sector is the basic unit of storage called a block ГҜвҖҡВ· The data is encoded as a magnetic pattern for each block ГҜвҖҡВ· When writing to disk, a variation in the current in the head produces a variation in magnetic field on the disk ГҜвҖҡВ· When reading from disk, a variation in magnetic field produces a variation in current through the head 5

2(c)(i) 1 mark for each correct row. Gate Input 1 Input 2 AND 1 1 NAND 0 0 1 0 1 0 XOR 0 1 1 0 NOR 0 0 4

2(c)(ii) 1 mark per bullet point ГҜвҖҡВ· A NAND B ГҜвҖҡВ· B XOR C ГҜвҖҡВ· OR ( A NAND B ) OR ( B XOR C ) 3 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 5 of 8 Question Answer Marks

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A teacher is writing examination papers on a laptop computer. The computer is connected to the internet. The teacher is concerned about the security and privacy of the papers. (a) State the difference between the security of data and the privacy of data. [1] (b) Identify and describe two threats to the data. Identify one security measure to protect against each threat. Each security measure must be different. Threat
mark_scheme_text:
3(a) Security prevents against loss while privacy prevents unauthorised access 1

3(b) 1 mark for identifying threat, 1 mark for description, 1 mark for security measure ( times 2 ) e.g. ГҜвҖҡВ· Malware ГҜвҖҡВ· Malicious software that replicates and can delete/damage the examination papers ГҜвҖҡВ· Install and run anti-malware ГҜвҖҡВ· Hacker/unauthorised access ГҜвҖҡВ· Illegal access in order to delete/damage the examination papers ГҜвҖҡВ· Use a firewall // strong passwords ГҜвҖҡВ· Spyware ГҜвҖҡВ· Software installed on the computer without the teacherГўвӮ¬в„ўs knowledge which records keystrokes and sends the data gathered about the examination papers to a third party ГҜвҖҡВ· Use a firewall / install and run anti-spyware / use a virtual (onscreen) keyboard 6 Question Answer Marks

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A teacher uses a relational database, MARKS , to store data about students and their test marks. The database has the following structure: STUDENT(StudentID, FirstName, LastName) TEST(TestID, Description, TotalMarks) STUDENT_TEST(StudentID, TestID, Mark) (a) Describe the advantages of using a relational database compared to a file-based approach. [4] (b) Give the highest level of Normal Form (NF) the database MARKS is in and justify your choice. Normal Form Justification [3] 8 9618/11/M/J/22 (c) (i) Sample data to be stored in the table STUDENT_TEST is shown. StudentID TestID Mark
mark_scheme_text:
4(a) 1 mark per bullet point to max 4 e.g. ГҜвҖҡВ· Reduced data redundancy // less repeated data ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰbecause each item of data is only stored once ГҜвҖҡВ· Maintains data consistency // improves data integrity ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰchanges in one table will automatically update in another ГҜвҖҡВ· ... linked data cannot be entered differently in two tables ГҜвҖҡВ· Program-data independence ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰchanges to the data do not require programs to be re-written ГҜвҖҡВ· Complex queries are easier to run ГҜвҖҡВ· Can provide different views ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰ.so users can only see specific aspects of the database 4

4(b) 1 mark for 3NF 1 mark per bullet for justification to max 2 ГҜвҖҡВ· There are no repeated attributes // it is already in 2NF ГҜвҖҡВ· Each field is fully dependent on the corresponding primary key // no partial dependencies ГҜвҖҡВ· No transitive dependencies 3 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 6 of 8 Question Answer Marks

4(c)(i) 1 mark per bullet point ГҜвҖҡВ· Create table, table name, opening and closing brackets ГҜвҖҡВ· StudentID and Mark as integer ГҜвҖҡВ· TestID as Varchar ГҜвҖҡВ· Primary key correctly set up ГҜвҖҡВ· Foreign keys correctly set up e.g. CREATE TABLE STUDENT_TEST ( StudentId INTEGER, TestID VARCHAR, Mark INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY(StudentID,TestID), FOREIGN KEY(TestID) REFERENCES TEST(TestID), FOREIGN KEY(StudentID) REFERENCES STUDENT(StudentID) ); 5

4(c)(ii) 1 mark for each point ГҜвҖҡВ· AVG(Mark) ГҜвҖҡВ· SELECT and FROM STUDENT_TEST ГҜвҖҡВ· WHERE clause e.g. SELECT AVG(Mark) FROM STUDENT_TEST WHERE TestID = "A7"; 3

4(d) 1 mark per bullet point to max 3 for validation e.g. ГҜвҖҡВ· range check to make sure it is between 0 and max marks ГҜвҖҡВ· presence check to make sure a mark is entered ГҜвҖҡВ· type check to make sure an integer value is entered 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 for verification e.g. ГҜвҖҡВ· double entry - enter the mark twice and the computer compares them ГҜвҖҡВ· visual check ГўвӮ¬вҖң manually compare the mark entered with the mark on the input document 4 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 7 of 8 Question Answer Marks

mark_scheme_text:
5(a) 1 mark for 1 correct line, 2 marks for 2 correct lines, 3 marks for 3 or 4 correct lines, 4 marks for all 5 correct lines IDE feature Description Context-sensitive prompt Executes one line of the program and then stops Dynamic syntax check Underlines or highlights statements that do not meet the rules of the language Breakpoint Outputs the contents of variables and data structures Single stepping Stops the code executing at a set line Report window Displays predictions of the code being entered 4

5(c) 1 mark per bullet point to max 3 ГҜвҖҡВ· Saves (programming/testing) time as code does not have to be written/re-written from scratch // code does not have to be tested ГҜвҖҡВ· Code is already tested so it is more robust/likely to work ГҜвҖҡВ· If there is an improvement in the library routine the program updates automatically ГҜвҖҡВ· can perform complex calculations that the programmer may be unable to do 3 9618/11 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 8 of 8 Question Answer Marks

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A computer system is designed using the basic Von Neumann model. (i) Describe the role of the registers in the Fetch-Execute (F-E) cycle. [5] (ii) Describe when interrupts are detected in the F-E cycle and how the interrupts are handled. Detected Handled [5] 13 9618/11/M/J/22 [Turn over (b) Identify one factor that can affect the performance of the computer system and state how it impacts the performance. Factor Impact [2] Question 6 continues on the next page. 14 9618/11/M/J/22 (c) The table shows part of the instruction set for a processor. The processor has one general purpose register, the Accumulator (ACC). Instruction Explanation Opcode Operand AND #n Bitwise AND operation of the contents of ACC with the operand XOR #n Bitwise XOR operation of the contents of ACC with the operand OR #n Bitwise OR operation of the contents of ACC with the operand LSL #n Bits in ACC are shifted logically n places to the left. Zeros are introduced on the right hand end LSR #n Bits in ACC are shifted logically n places to the right. Zeros are introduced on the left hand end # denotes a denary number, e.g. #123 (i) Complete the register to show the result after the instruction AND #2 is executed. Register before: 0 1 1 0 1 1 0
mark_scheme_text:
6(a)(i) 1 mark per bullet point to max 5 ГҜвҖҡВ· The Program Counter (PC) holds the address of the next instruction ГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰand the contents are incremented / changed to the next address each cycle ГҜвҖҡВ· The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address to fetch the data (from the PC) ГҜвҖҡВ· The Memory Data Register (MDR) holds the data at the address in MAR ГҜвҖҡВ· The instruction is transferred to Current Instruction Register (CIR) for decoding and execution 5

6(a)(ii) 1 mark for detection ГҜвҖҡВ· At the start/end of a FE cycle 1 mark for handling to max 4 ГҜвҖҡВ· Priority is checked ГҜвҖҡВ· If lower priority than current process continue with F-E cycle ГҜвҖҡВ· If higher priority than current process ГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰ state of current process is / registers are stored on stack ГҜвҖҡВ· Location / type of interrupt identified... ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰappropriate ISR is called to handle the interrupt ГҜвҖҡВ· When ISR finished, check for further interrupts (of high priority) / return to step 1 ГҜвҖҡВ· Otherwise load data from stack and continue with process 5

6(b) 1 mark for factor 1 mark for impact e.g. ГҜвҖҡВ· Clock speedГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰhigher clock speed means more FE cycles per second ГҜвҖҡВ· Number of coresГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰmeans more instructions can be carried out simultaneously ГҜвҖҡВ· Bus width ГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰallows the transfer of more data each time // allows more memory locations to be directly accessed ГҜвҖҡВ· Cache ГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰ the higher capacity the more frequently used instructions it can store for fast access 2

6(c)(i) 0000 0000 1

6(c)(ii) 0110 1101 1

6(c)(iii) 1101 0000 1

Term Definition Pixel Bit depth Image resolution File header The number of pixels wide by the number of pixels high The smallest identifiable component of an image Stores data about the image file, e.g. file format, number of bits per pixel, file size The number of bits used to represent each colour [3] (b) The following section of a bitmap image is 10 pixels wide and 5 pixels high. In this example, each colour is represented by a letter, e.g. B is blue. (i) Identify the smallest number of bits that can be used to represent each colour in the complete bitmap image. [1] 3 9618/12/M/J/22 [Turn over (ii) Calculate an estimate for the file size of the section of the bitmap image shown, giving your answer in bytes. Use your answer from part (b)(i) . Show your working. Working Answer bytes [2] (c) Describe how changing the colour depth of an image affects its file size. [2] (d) The first row of pixels in the image from part (b) is shown: B B B B B B B B B B Explain how this row of pixels can be compressed using lossless compression. [2] 4 9618/12/M/J/22 Write the bit manipulation instruction that would change the binary integer in ACC to: 1 1 1 1 1 1 1 Opcode Operand [2] (b) The ACC currently contains the following positive binary integer: 0 1 1 0 0 1 0 Write the bit manipulation instruction that would change the binary integer in ACC to: 1 0 0 1 1 0 1

1(c) 1 mark per point ГҜвҖҡВ· Increasing the colour depth results in increased file size // Decreasing the colour depth results in smaller file size ГҜвҖҡВ· Increasing the colour depth means more bits per pixel and hence more data stored // Decreasing the colour depth means fewer bits per pixel and hence less data stored 2

subject: A-Level Computer Science
question_text: A database, FILMS , stores information about films and actors. Part of the database is shown: ACTOR(ActorID, FirstName, LastName, DateOfBirth) FILM_FACT(FilmID, FilmTitle, ReleaseDate, Category) FILM_ACTOR(ActorID, FilmID) (a) Complete the entity-relationship (E-R) diagram. ACTOR FILM_FACT FILM_ACTOR [2] (b) A composite primary key consists of two or more attributes that together form the primary key. Explain why the table FILM_ACTOR has a composite primary key. [2] 9 9618/12/M/J/22 [Turn over (c) Complete the SQL script to return the IDs of all the actors in the film with the title Cinderella. SELECT FROM FILM_ACTOR INNER JOIN ON FILM_FACT.FilmID = WHERE FILM_FACT.FilmTitle = ; [4] (d) Write an SQL script to count the number of films that were released in January 2022. [3] 10 9618/12/M/J/22 (e) A Database Management System (DBMS) is used to create and manipulate the database. Complete the descriptions of the features and tools found in a DBMS using the given terms. Not all terms will be used. Boolean data dictionary data redundancy field names input interface logical schema normalisation operating system output primary keys query structure A DBMS provides data management. This includes the development of a that stores information about the data stored, such as and . The uses methods, such as an E-R diagram, to show the structure of the database and its relationships. The processor allows a user to perform searches to find specific data. The DBMS also provides a developer that allows the user to create tables, forms and reports. [6] 11 9618/12/M/J/22 [Turn over
mark_scheme_text:
5(a) 1 mark for each correct relationship 2

5(b) 1 mark per point ГҜвҖҡВ· Neither key uniquely identifies each tuple by itself ГҜвҖҡВ· One actor cannot appear in the same film twice so together they are unique 2

5(c) 1 mark per correct entry SELECT FILM_ACTOR.ActorID / ActorID FROM FILM_ACTOR INNER JOIN FILM_FACT ON FILM_FACT.FilmID = FILM_ACTOR.FilmID WHERE FILM_FACT.FilmTitle = "Cinderella" ; 4

5(d) 1 mark per point ГҜвҖҡВ· COUNT and correct fieldname ГҜвҖҡВ· SELECT and FROM statements, including the table name in FROM ГҜвҖҡВ· WHERE statement e.g. SELECT COUNT(FilmID) FROM FILM_FACT WHERE ReleaseDate >= #01/01/2022# AND ReleaseDate <= #31/01/2022#; // WHERE ReleaseDate BETWEEN #01/01/2022# AND #31/01/2022#; // WHERE ReleaseDate = ГўвӮ¬Е“January 2022ГўвӮ¬Вқ; 3 ACTOR FILM_FACT FILM_ACTOR 9618/12 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 7 of 9 Question Answer Marks

5(e) 1 mark for each correctly completed term ГҜвҖҡВ· data dictionary ГҜвҖҡВ· field names // primary keys ГҜвҖҡВ· primary keys //field names ГҜвҖҡВ· logical schema ГҜвҖҡВ· query ГҜвҖҡВ· interface A DBMS provides data management. This includes the development of a data dictionary that stores information about the data stored, such as field names and primary keys . The logical schema uses methods such as an E-R diagram to show the structure of the database and its relationships. The query processor allows a user to perform searches to find specific data. The DBMS also provides a developer interface that allows the user to create tables, forms and reports. 6 Question Answer Marks

question_text: A programmer uses language translators when writing and testing a program. (a) Describe the operation of a compiler. [2] (b) Describe the operation of an interpreter. mark_scheme_text:

question_text: A company wants to store data about its employees in a computer system. The owner of the company wants to ensure the security and integrity of the data. (a) (i) State why data needs to be kept secure. [1] (ii) One way the data stored in a computer can be kept secure is by using back-up software. Give two other ways the data stored in a computer can be kept secure. 1 2 [2] (b) The data about the employees is currently stored on paper. The data needs to be transferred into the computer system. Data validation and verification are used to help maintain the integrity of the data. (i) Identify and describe one method of data verification that can be used when transferring the data from paper to the computer. Method Description [2] (ii) The company needs to transfer the date of birth of each employee into the computer system. Give one example of how each of the following data validation rules can be used to validate the date of birth when it is entered into the system. Range check Presence check Length check [3] 11 9618/13/M/J/22 [Turn over (iii) Explain why the data in the system may not be correct even after validating and verifying the data. [2] 12 9618/13/M/J/22
mark_scheme_text:
5(a)(i) 1 mark from ГҜвҖҡВ· To stop the data being lost / corrupted / amended ГҜвҖҡВ· To make sure it can be recovered ГҜвҖҡВ· To prevent unauthorised access 1

5(a)(ii) 1 mark each e.g. ГҜвҖҡВ· Install / run a firewall ГҜвҖҡВ· Up to date Anti-virus / anti-malware ГҜвҖҡВ· (Username and ) ( strong) password ГҜвҖҡВ· Encryption ГҜвҖҡВ· Access rights 2

5(b)(i) 1 mark each ГҜвҖҡВ· Visual check ГҜвҖҡВ· Manually compare the data entered with the original (document) ГҜвҖҡВ· Double entry ГҜвҖҡВ· Enter the data twice and the system compares them to see if they are the same 2

5(b)(ii) 1 mark each e.g. Range check: ГҜвҖҡВ· Make sure it is after and before a specific date // e.g. between 1900 and today's date // check month is between 1 and 12 // check day is between 1 and month end Presence check: ГҜвҖҡВ· Make sure the date of birth has been entered Length check: ГҜвҖҡВ· Make sure there are at least 1 for day, 1 for month, 2/4 for year // must be 8 characters 3 5(b(iii) 1 mark per bullet point to max 2 ГҜвҖҡВ· Validation checks data is reasonable/within bounds it does not check that accurate data has been entered ГҜвҖҡВ· Verification checks if the data matches the data given it does not check if the original data is accurate 2 9618/13 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 8 of 10 Question Answer Marks

question_text: A string is a palindrome if it reads the same forwards as backwards. The following strings are examples of palindromes: "Racecar" "madam" "12344321" Upper-case and lower-case characters need to be treated the same. For example, 'A' is equivalent to 'a'. (a) A function IsPalindrome() will take a string parameter. The function will return TRUE if the string is a palindrome and will return FALSE if the string is not a palindrome. Write pseudocode for IsPalindrome() . 12 9618/21/M/J/22 [7] 13 9618/21/M/J/22 [Turn over (b) Strings may consist of several words separated by spaces. For example, the string "never odd or even" becomes a palindrome if the spaces are removed. The program flowchart represents an algorithm to produce a string OutString by removing all spaces from a string InString . START END Set Index to
mark_scheme_text:
7(a) FUNCTION IsPalindrome(InString : STRING) RETURNS BOOLEAN DECLARE IsPal : BOOLEAN DECLARE Index, Num : INTEGER DECLARE CharA, CharB : CHAR IsPal ГҜвҖҡВ¬ TRUE Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 Num ГҜвҖҡВ¬ INT(LENGTH(InString) / 2) WHILE Index <= Num AND IsPal = TRUE CharA ГҜвҖҡВ¬ MID(InString, Index, 1) CharB ГҜвҖҡВ¬ MID(Instring, LENGTH(Instring) ГўвӮ¬вҖң Index + 1, 1) IF UCASE(CharA) <> UCASE(CharB) THEN IsPal ГҜвҖҡВ¬ FALSE // RETURN FALSE ENDIF Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Index + 1 ENDWHILE RETURN IsPal // RETURN TRUE ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Functions header including parameter, ending and return type 2 Calculation of number of pairs to match (length or half length) 3 Loop for half or whole string 4 ГўвӮ¬ВҰExtracting characters to compare // create reverse string 5 Convert characters to same case 6 Check for mismatch of characters inside loop / test for mismatch after loop for reversed string 7 Returning Boolean in both cases 7 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 9 of 12 Question Answer Marks

question_text: A program allows a user to save passwords used to login to websites. A stored password is inserted automatically when the user logs into the corresponding website. A student is developing a program to generate a password. The password will be of a fixed format, consisting of three groups of four alphanumeric characters. The groups are separated by the hyphen character '-'. An example of a password is: "FxAf-3haV-Tq49" A global 2D array Secret of type STRING stores the passwords together with the website domain name where they are used. Secret contains 1000 elements organised as 500 rows by 2 columns. Unused elements contain the empty string ( "" ). These may occur anywhere in the array. An example of a part of the array is: Array element Value Secret[27, 1] " Secret[27, 2] " ГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕё " Secret[28, 1] " Secret[28, 2] " ГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕё " Note: ГўвӮ¬Вў For security, passwords are stored in an encrypted form, shown as " ГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕёГҜвҖҡЕё " in the example. ГўвӮ¬Вў The passwords cannot be used without being decrypted. ГўвӮ¬Вў Assume that the encrypted form of a password will not be an empty string. The programmer has started to define program modules as follows: Module Description RandomChar() ГўвӮ¬Вў Generates a single random character from within one of the following ranges: ГўвҖ”вҖ№ 'a' to 'z' ГўвҖ”вҖ№ 'A' to 'Z' ГўвҖ”вҖ№ '0' to '9' ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the character Encrypt() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes a password as a parameter of type string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the encrypted form of the password as a string Decrypt() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes an encrypted password as a parameter of type string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the decrypted form of the password as a string For reference, relevant ASCII values are as follows: Character range ASCII range 'a' to 'z' 97 to 122 'A' to 'Z' 65 to
mark_scheme_text:
8(a) FUNCTION RandomChar() RETURNS CHAR DECLARE ThisRange : INTEGER DECLARE ThisChar : CHAR //First select the range ThisRange ГҜвҖҡВ¬ INT(RAND(3)) + 1 // 1 to 3 CASE OF ThisRange 1: ThisChar ГҜвҖҡВ¬ CHR(INT(RAND(26) + 65)) // 65 to 90: 'A' to 'Z' ThisChar ГҜвҖҡВ¬ LCASE(ThisChar) // 'a' to 'z' 2: ThisChar ГҜвҖҡВ¬ CHR(INT(RAND(26) + 65)) // 65 to 90: A to Z 3: ThisChar ГҜвҖҡВ¬ NUM_TO_STR(INT(RAND(10)) // '0' to '9' ENDCASE RETURN ThisChar ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Generation of any integer random number 2 Randomly decide which of the three ranges to select 3 Selection structure based on range 4 One alphanumeric character range correct 5 All alphanumeric character ranges correct 6 Return ThisChar , following a reasonable attempt to generate a character in each range 6 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 11 of 12 Question Answer Marks

8(b) FUNCTION FindPassword(Name: STRING) RETURNS STRING DECLARE Index : INTEGER DECLARE Password : STRING Password ГҜвҖҡВ¬ "" Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 WHILE Password = "" AND Index <= 500 IF Secret[Index, 1] = Name THEN Password ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Decrypt(Secret[Index, 2]) ELSE Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Index + 1 ENDIF ENDWHILE IF Password = "" THEN OUTPUT "Domain name not found" ENDIF RETURN Password ENDFUNCTION Mark as follows: 1 Declare all local variables used, attempted solution has to be reasonable 2 Conditional loop while not found and not end of array 3 Compare value of element in column 1 with parameter passed into function 4 ...and use Decrypt () with element in column 2 as parameter 5 ГўвӮ¬ВҰuse the return value of Decrypt() 6 Output warning message if parameter not found 7 Return STRING value 7

8(c) One mark for the name, one for the description Name: ГҜвҖҡВ· Stub testing Description: ГҜвҖҡВ· A simple module is written to replace each of the modules. ГҜвҖҡВ· The simple module will return an expected value // will output a message to show they have been called 3

8(d) Accept one example of a valid password to Max 2 One mark for each password example that breaks one of the rules due to: ГҜвҖҡВ· Length too long // length too short ГҜвҖҡВ· Invalid character ГҜвҖҡВ· Incorrect grouping (including number of hyphens) ГҜвҖҡВ· Duplicated characters 2 9618/21 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 12 of 12 Question Answer Marks

8(e) One mark for each part: ГҜвҖҡВ· Generate a random integer divisible by 3 ГҜвҖҡВ· Split range into 1/3 and set as numeric ГҜвҖҡВ· Else alphabetic character 3

The calling program will pass the number of lines to be output as well as the name of the text file. The number of lines could be any value from 1 to 30. It can be assumed that the file contains at least the number of lines passed. Outline three changes that would be needed. 1 2 3 [3] 8 9618/22/M/J/22
mark_scheme_text:
4(a) PROCEDURE LastLines(ThisFile : STRING) DECLARE ThisLine, LineX, LineY, LineZ : STRING OPENFILE ThisFile FOR READ LineY ГҜвҖҡВ¬ "" LineZ ГҜвҖҡВ¬ "" WHILE NOT EOF(ThisFile) READFILE Thisfile, ThisLine // read a line LineX ГҜвҖҡВ¬ LineY LineY ГҜвҖҡВ¬ LineZ LineZ ГҜвҖҡВ¬ ThisLine ENDWHILE CLOSEFILE ThisFile OUTPUT LineX OUTPUT LineY OUTPUT LineZ ENDPROCEDURE Marks as follows to Max 6 : 1 Procedure heading (including parameter) and ending 2 Declaration of local variables for three lines AND File OPEN in READ mode AND CLOSE 3 Loop until EOF(ThisFile) 4 Read line from file... in a loop 5 Attempt at a shuffleГўвӮ¬ВҰ in a loop 6 Correctly shuffle LineX , LineY and LineZ in a loop 7 OUTPUT the three lines in correct sequence, following reasonable attempt 6 9618/22 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 7 of 14 Question Answer Marks

4(a) Alternative (using two loops): PROCEDURE LastLines(ThisFile : STRING) DECLARE ThisLine, LineX, LineY, LineZ : STRING DECLARE Count, Count2 : INTEGER Count ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 0 OPENFILE ThisFile FOR READ WHILE NOT EOF(ThisFile) READFILE Thisfile, ThisLine // read a line Count ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Count + 1 ENDWHILE CLOSEFILE ThisFile OPENFILE ThisFile FOR READ FOR Count2 ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 TO Count - 3 READFILE Thisfile, ThisLine // read a line NEXT Count2 READFILE Thisfile, LineX READFILE Thisfile, LineY READFILE Thisfile, LineZ OUTPUT LineX OUTPUT LineY OUTPUT LineZ CLOSEFILE ThisFile ENDPROCEDURE Marks as follows to Max 6 : 1 Procedure heading (including parameter) and ending 2 Declaration of local variables for three lines AND (at least one) File OPEN in READ mode AND CLOSE 3 Loop until EOF(ThisFile) 4 Read line from file and increment Count in a loop 5 Two separate loops, closing and re-opening the file between loops 6 Read Count - 3 lines from the file 7 OUTPUT the last three lines in correct sequence, following reasonable attempt 9618/22 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 8 of 14 Question Answer Marks

ГўвӮ¬Вў The passwords cannot be used without being decrypted. ГўвӮ¬Вў You may assume that the encrypted form of a password will NOT be an empty string. The programmer has started to define program modules as follows: Module Description Exists() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes two parameters: ГўвҖ”вҖ№ a string ГўвҖ”вҖ№ a character ГўвӮ¬Вў Performs a case-sensitive search for the character in the string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns TRUE if the character occurs in the string, otherwise returns FALSE Encrypt() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes a password as a parameter of type string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the encrypted form of the password as a string Decrypt() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes an encrypted password as a parameter of type string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the decrypted form of the password as a string Note: in a case-sensitive comparison, 'a' is not the same as 'A'. 15 9618/22/M/J/22 [Turn over (a) Write pseudocode for the module Exists() . [5] 16 9618/22/M/J/22 (b) A new module SearchDuplicates() will: ГўвӮ¬Вў search for the first password that occurs more than once in the array and output a message each time a duplicate is found. For example, if the same password was used for the three websites ThisWebsite.com, website27.net and websiteZ99.org, then the following messages will be output: "Password for ThisWebsite.com also used for website27.net" "Password for ThisWebsite.com also used for websiteZ99.org" ГўвӮ¬Вў end once all messages have been output. The module will output a message if no duplicates are found. For example: "No duplicate passwords found" Write efficient pseudocode for the module SearchDuplicates() . Encrypt() and Decrypt() functions have been written. Note : It is necessary to decrypt each password before checking its value. 17 9618/22/M/J/22 [Turn over [8] 18 9618/22/M/J/22 (c) A password has a fixed format, consisting of three groups of four alphanumeric characters, separated by the hyphen character '-'. An example of a password is: "FxAf-3haV-Tq49" Each password must: ГўвӮ¬Вў be 14 characters long ГўвӮ¬Вў be organised as three groups of four alphanumeric characters. The groups are separated by hyphen characters ГўвӮ¬Вў not include any duplicated characters, except for the hyphen characters. An algorithm is needed for a new function GeneratePassword() , which will generate and return a password in this format. Assume that the following modules have already been written:

8(a) FUNCTION Exists(ThisString : STRING, Search : CHAR) RETURNS BOOLEAN DECLARE Found : BOOLEAN DECLARE Index : INTEGER Found ГҜвҖҡВ¬ FALSE Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 WHILE Found = FALSE AND Index <= LENGTH(ThisString) IF MID(ThisString, Index, 1) = Search THEN Found ГҜвҖҡВ¬ TRUE ELSE Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Index + 1 ENDIF ENDWHILE RETURN Found ENDFUNCTION Marks as follows (Conditional loop solution): 1 Conditional loop while character not found and not end of string 2 Extract a char in a loop 3 Compare with parameter without case conversion in a loop 4 If match found, set termination logic in a loop 5 Return BOOLEAN value ALTERNATIVE (Using Count-controlled loop): FOR Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 TO LENGTH(ThisString) IF MID(ThisString, Index, 1) = Search THEN RETURN TRUE ENDIF NEXT Index RETURN FALSE Marks as follows (Count-controlled loop variant): 1 Loop for length of ThisString (allow from 0 or 1) 2 Extract a char in a loop 3 Compare with parameter without case conversion in a loop 4 If match found, immediate RETURN of TRUE 5 Return FALSE after the loop // Return Boolean if no immediate RETURN 5 9618/22 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 13 of 14 Question Answer Marks

8(b) PROCEDURE SearchDuplicates() DECLARE IndexA, IndexB : INTEGER DECLARE ThisPassword, ThisValue : STRING DECLARE Duplicates : BOOLEAN Duplicates ГҜвҖҡВ¬ FALSE IndexA ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 WHILE Duplicates = FALSE AND IndexA < 500 ThisValue ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Secret[IndexA, 2] IF ThisValue <> "" THEN ThisPassword ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Decrypt(ThisValue) FOR IndexB ГҜвҖҡВ¬ IndexA + 1 TO 500 // IF Secret[IndexB, 2] <> "" THEN IF Decrypt(Secret[IndexB, 2]) = ThisPassword THEN OUTPUT "Password for " & Secret[IndexA, 1] & "also used for " & Secret[IndexB, 1] Duplicates ГҜвҖҡВ¬ TRUE ENDIF ENDIF NEXT IndexB ENDIF IndexA ГҜвҖҡВ¬ IndexA + 1 ENDWHILE IF Duplicates = FALSE THEN OUTPUT "No duplicate passwords found" ENDIF ENDPROCEDURE Marks as follows to Max 8 : 1. (Any) conditional loop... 2. ... from 1 to 499 while (attempt at) no duplicate 3. Skip unused password 4. Use Decrypt() and assign return value to ThisPassword 5. Inner loop from outer loop index + 1 to 500 searching for duplicates 6. Compare ThisPassword with subsequent passwords ( after use of Decrypt() ) 7. If match found, set outer loop termination 8. and attempt an Output message giving duplicate 9. Output 'No duplicate passwords found' message if no duplicates found after the loop 8 9618/22 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 14 of 14 Question Answer Marks

An example of a password is: "FxAf-3hzV-Aq49" A valid password: ГўвӮ¬Вў must be 14 characters long ГўвӮ¬Вў must be organised as three groups of four alphanumeric characters. The groups are separated by hyphen characters ГўвӮ¬Вў may include duplicated characters, provided these appear in different groups. The programmer has started to define program modules as follows: Module Description RandomChar() ГўвӮ¬Вў Generates a single random character from within one of the following ranges: ГўвҖ”вҖ№ 'a' to 'z' ГўвҖ”вҖ№ 'A' to 'Z' ГўвҖ”вҖ№ '0' to '9' ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the character Exists() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes two parameters: ГўвҖ”вҖ№ a string ГўвҖ”вҖ№ a character ГўвӮ¬Вў Performs a case-sensitive search for the character in the string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns TRUE if the character occurs in the string, otherwise returns FALSE Generate() ГўвӮ¬Вў Generates a valid password ГўвӮ¬Вў Uses RandomChar() and Exists() ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the password Note: in a case-sensitive comparison, 'a' is not the same as 'A'. 15 9618/23/M/J/22 [Turn over (a) Write pseudocode for the module Generate() . [7] 16 9618/23/M/J/22 (b) A global 2D array Secret of type STRING stores the passwords together with the website domain name where they are used. ГўвӮ¬Вў The passwords cannot be used without being decrypted. ГўвӮ¬Вў You may assume that the encrypted form of a password will not be an empty string. Additional modules are defined as follows: Module Description Encrypt() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes a password as a string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the encrypted form of the password as a string Decrypt() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes an encrypted password as a string ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns the decrypted form of the password as a string FindPassword() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes a website domain name as a string ГўвӮ¬Вў Searches for the website domain name in the array Secret ГўвӮ¬Вў If the website domain name is found, the decrypted password is returned ГўвӮ¬Вў If the website domain name is not found, an empty string is returned AddPassword() ГўвӮ¬Вў Takes two parameters as strings: a website domain name and a password ГўвӮ¬Вў Searches for the website domain name in the array Secret and if not found, adds the website domain name and the encrypted password to the array ГўвӮ¬Вў Returns TRUE if the website domain name and encrypted password are added to the array, otherwise returns FALSE The first three modules have been written. 17 9618/23/M/J/22 [Turn over Write pseudoc
mark_scheme_text:
9(a) FUNCTION Generate() RETURNS STRING DECLARE Password, Group : STRING DECLARE NextChar : CHAR DECLARE ACount, BCount : INTEGER CONSTANT HYPHEN = '-' Password ГҜвҖҡВ¬ "" FOR ACount ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 TO 3 Group ГҜвҖҡВ¬ "" FOR BCount ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 TO 4 REPEAT NextChar ГҜвҖҡВ¬ RandomChar() UNTIL Exists(Group, NextChar) = FALSE Group ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Group & NextChar NEXT BCount Password ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Password & Group & HYPHEN NEXT ACount Password ГҜвҖҡВ¬ LEFT(Password, 14) // remove final hyphen RETURN Password ENDFUNCTION Marks as follows to Max 7 : 1 Declaration and initialisation of Password as STRING 2 Outer loop for three groups / until password is complete // three group loops 3 Attempt to use of both RandomChar() and Exists() in a loop 4 (Inner) loop for 4 characters in a group // note every 4 chars in a loop 5 Conditional loop until char is unique 6 Concatenating unique character to Group in a loop 7 Concatenate Group / random character to Password in a loop 8 (Attempt to) insert hyphens between groups (or removing later) and Return Password 7 9618/23 Cambridge International AS & A Level ГўвӮ¬вҖң Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 11 of 11 Question Answer Marks

9(b) FUNCTION AddPassword(Name, Password : STRING) RETURNS BOOLEAN DECLARE Index : INTEGER DECLARE Added : BOOLEAN Added ГҜвҖҡВ¬ FALSE Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ 1 IF FindPassword(Name) = "" THEN // Domain name not in // array WHILE Added = FALSE AND Index <= 500 IF Secret[Index, 1] = "" THEN Secret[Index, 1] ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Name Secret[Index, 2] ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Encrypt(Password) Added ГҜвҖҡВ¬ TRUE ELSE Index ГҜвҖҡВ¬ Index + 1 ENDIF ENDWHILE ENDIF RETURN Added ENDFUNCTION Marks as follows: 1 Check that the website domain name isn't already in array using FindPassword() / linear search, otherwise: 2 (Conditional) loop while not added and not end of array 3 Check for unused element by testing value in column 1 in a loop 4 If unused, write parameter values to column 1 and 2 and set flag / variable 5 ...having used Encrypt() on the password 6 Return BOOLEAN value (correctly in all cases) 6

9(c) One mark per point to Max 3 . Solution based on field length: ГҜвҖҡВ· Convert the length of the website domain name (either field) ГўвӮ¬ВҰ ГҜвҖҡВ· ГўвӮ¬ВҰ to a string of fixed length ГҜвҖҡВ· Form a string by concatenate this string with the other two (and write as one line of the file) Solution based on use of separator character: ГҜвҖҡВ· Select a (separator) character that cannot occur in the domain name (e.g. space) ГҜвҖҡВ· Create a string from the domain name followed by the separator ГҜвҖҡВ· ...Concatenate the encrypted password (and write as one line of the file) 3
